texto: If Elirena was a hero, if what she did is heroism, then that's just too bad. Let them call me a traitor and a coward. Because I, Yarpen Zigrin, coward, traitor and renegade, state that we should not kill each other. I state that we ought to live. Live in such a way that we don't, later, have to ask anyone for forgiveness. The Heroic Elirena... She had to ask. Forgive me, she begged, forgive me. To hell with that! It's better to die than to live in the knowledge that you've done something that needs forgiveness.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Andrzej Sapkowski sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “If Elirena was a hero, if what she did is heroism, then that's just too bad. Let them call me a t…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a motivacao, inspiracional, life-lessons, útil em momentos de inspiracao e life-lessons.

texto: Treaties are like marriage: they aren't entered in to with the thought of betrayal, and once they're concluded one shouldn't be suspicious. texto: You've a right to believe that we're governed by Nature and the hidden Force within her. texto: Why didn't you become a sorcerer, Geralt? Weren't you ever attracted by the Art? Be honest.''I will. I was.''Why, then, didn't you follow the voice of that attraction?''I decided it would be wiser to follow the voice of good sense.''Meaning?''Years of practice in the witcher's trade have taught me not to bite off more than I can chew. Do you know, Vilgefortz, I once knew a dwarf, who, as a child, dreamed of being an elf. What do you think; would he have become one had he followed the voice of attraction?

texto: People," Geralt turned his head, "like to invent monsters and monstrosities. Then they seem less monstrous themselves. When they get blind-drunk, cheat, steal, beat their wives, starve an old woman, when they kill a trapped fox with an axe or riddle the last existing unicorn with arrows, they like to think that the Bane entering cottages at daybreak is more monstrous than they are. They feel better then. They find it easier to live.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Andrzej Sapkowski: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “People," Geralt turned his head, "like to invent monsters and monstrosities. texto: It's an invention, a fairy tale devoid of any sense, like all the legends in which good spirits and fortune tellers fulfill wishes. Stories like that are made up by poor simpletons, who can't even dream of fulfilling their wishes and desires themselves. I'm pleased you're not one of them, Geralt of Rivia. It makes you closer in spirit to me. If I want something, I don't dream of it—I act. And I always get what I want.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Andrzej Sapkowski: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. seo.titleSeo: "It's an invention, a fairy tale devoid of any sense,…" — Andrzej Sapkowski | Metamensagem
seo.descriptionSeo: Metáfora atribuída a Andrzej Sapkowski: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “It's an invention, a fairy tale…

frase_original: It's an invention, a fairy tale devoid of any sense, like all the legends in which good spirits and fortune tellers fulfill wishes. Stories like that are made up by poor simpletons, who can't even dream of fulfilling their wishes and desires themselves. I'm pleased you're not one of them, Geralt of Rivia. It makes you closer in spirit to me. If I want something, I don't dream of it—I act. And I always get what I want.

texto: The sword of destiny has two edges. You are one of them.
explicacao: Citação de Andrzej Sapkowski sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “The sword of destiny has two edges. You are one of them.” articula reflexao, destiny, sword em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.
